marriages.notes: Per EH 2449: m1. Nov 9, 1857 Annah Baxter, daughter of Luther + Oledine (Rowe) Baxter of Cornwall VT, b. June 7, 1834 Cornwall VT. d. Nov 8, 1869 Castleton VT (age 35). No documented children — EH 2449 says 'CHILDREN, HOOKER. All by second marriage.'

Per EH 2449 + WikiTree: m2. Sept 6, 1871 Elizabeth Mary Muzzy (1844 India - 1912 Dorchester MA, age 68). Daughter of Rev. Clarendon Fay Muzzy + Samantha Bowen Robbins Muzzy (missionaries in India where mother died); ADOPTED by uncle Prof. Rensselaer D.C. Robbins + wife Sarah (Stuart) Robbins → known by Robbins surname per EH. Six children: Bessie Robbins Hooker (1872-1960 age 88), Stuart Van Rensselaer Hooker (*1874-1948 age 74 — surgeon), Emily Griswold Hooker (1875-1936 age 61), Edward Clarendon Hooker (*1876-1938 age 62 — m. Ruth Fairchild), Mary Stuart Hooker (1878-1961 age 83), David Ashley Hooker (1879-1951 age 72).

education.notes: Per EH 2449: 'graduated from Middlebury College in 1855.'
Per EH 2449: 'and from Andover Theological Seminary in 1861.'

careers.notes: Per EH 2449: 'He preached a while in Massachusetts.'

Per EH 2449 (published 1909): 'is now President of Rollins College, Winter Park, Florida.' Predecessor: Charles Grandison Fairchild (2nd president 1893-1895/1898). Two Rollins College Presidents are connected to the Hooker tree: Edward Payson Hooker (this entry) and Charles G. Fairchild (X-easter_egg, future father-in-law of Edward Payson's son Edward Clarendon — they married in 1907).

notable.notable_blurb: President of Rollins College Winter Park

narrative_blocks.header: He rose from country pulpit to college president
narrative_blocks.body: A Middlebury graduate trained at Andover Seminary, Hooker served as a Congregational minister in Massachusetts before taking the presidency of Rollins College in Florida. He led the young Florida college around the turn of the century.

narrative_blocks.header: His son married a fellow president's daughter
narrative_blocks.body: Hooker's son Edward married Ruth Fairchild, daughter of another Rollins College president, joining two academic families. Another son, Stuart, became a surgeon who had studied at Rollins.

narrative_blocks.header: His second wife was born in India
narrative_blocks.body: Hooker's second wife, Elizabeth, had been born in India to missionary parents; her mother died there, and an uncle raised her. Their six children all came of this second marriage.

cross_connections.evidence: Charles G. Fairchild was 2nd President of Rollins College (1893-1895/1898). Edward Payson Hooker was a later Rollins President (per EH 1909). Their children married each other 1907 — Edward Clarendon Hooker (H03917) m. Ruth Fairchild — connecting two Rollins presidential lines by marriage.
cross_connections.display_label: Both Rollins College presidents; their children married 1907
